Content.p: Prepared for: Michael — Software Development Team
An LLM-Powered Game Master & Lore Engine for World of Darkness Tabletop Campaigns

The darkness does not yield its secrets easily. The White Wolf Storyteller AI is a system born from shadows — an intelligent, lore-aware engine that breathes life into the World of Darkness from the vantage point of the digital age. This section establishes what the system is, how its components relate to one another, and what technologies anchor its foundations.

The Storyteller AI is a large language model (LLM) powered backend system that functions simultaneously as Game Master, lore oracle, NPC voice engine, and narrative continuity engine for tabletop roleplaying sessions within the World of Darkness universe — encompassing Vampire: The Masquerade, Werewolf: The Apocalypse, Mage: The Ascension, Changeling: The Dreaming, Hunter: The Reckoning, Wraith: The Oblivion, and adjacent splats.

Unlike a static chatbot, the Storyteller AI maintains rich, persistent narrative state across sessions, retrieves accurate lore from ingested official source books, tracks character sheets and NPC relationships, and adapts its voice and pacing to the emotional tenor of the chronicle. It speaks not as a machine querying a database, but as an ancient, world-weary narrator who has witnessed the rise and fall of Kindred princes, the howl of the Garou at the dying Gauntlet, and the slow unraveling of consensual reality under Mage hands.
The system supports three distinct operational modes, each targeting a different play context:
AI acts as full GM for a single player running a personal chronicle
Full narration, offers 3 choices per scene, tracks all state autonomously
AI manages a full table of 2–8 players, coordinating spotlight and character responses
Multi-character tracking, turn management, inter-PC conflict handling
AI acts as co-GM whispering suggestions, lore lookups, and encounter ideas to a human GM
Ephemeral suggestions, never narrates directly, bracket-annotated outputs

The system ingests official White Wolf and Onyx Path PDFs — rulebooks, lore supplements, chronicles, and setting sourcebooks — through a dedicated processing pipeline. Raw PDF text is extracted, cleaned of OCR artifacts, chunked into semantically coherent passages, embedded into a vector space, and stored in ChromaDB. Metadata (source book, page, chapter, splat relevance) is persisted to PostgreSQL alongside each embedding reference. At query time, the retrieval layer performs semantic similarity search to surface the most relevant lore chunks and injects them into the LLM prompt context — grounding AI narration in canonical material rather than hallucinated lore.

The Storyteller AI must never fabricate canonical lore not present in the ingested source material. All supernatural power descriptions, faction histories, and mechanical rules must be grounded in retrieved chunks. Unfounded invention erodes player trust and breaks immersion. The retrieval layer is not optional — it is the difference between a Storyteller and a liar.
The following table replaces the original text diagram with a Word-friendly architecture map showing the major components, their responsibilities, and the primary data flows between them.
White Wolf Storyteller AI — System Architecture v1.0
Players and GM interact through slash commands and channel messages.
app/main.py, api/v1/routes, lifespan context, CORS, auth middleware
Coordinates narration requests, lore lookups, session management, and ingestion endpoints.
LLM Layer (OpenAI GPT-4o or local adapter)
Builds prompts, manages state, retrieves lore, and produces narration responses.
Stores session state, character data, lore chunks, embeddings, and asynchronous ingestion jobs.

Primary Request Flow: Player Input → Discord Bot → FastAPI Backend → Storyteller Engine → State Machine / Vector Retrieval / Prompt Builder → LLM Layer → Narration Response → FastAPI Backend → Discord Bot → Player
Primary Ingestion Flow: PDF Upload → FastAPI Backend → Celery Task → PDF Parser → Chunk Embedder → ChromaDB + PostgreSQL
Discord Bot sends player input over httpx to FastAPI, which invokes the Storyteller Engine to assemble context, retrieve lore, update state, and return narration.
The Storyteller Engine queries the vector store for relevant lore chunks and combines the results with session state before sending the final prompt to the LLM layer.
Narrative updates, character changes, NPC activity, and event logs are written through SQLAlchemy to PostgreSQL or SQLite.
Uploaded sourcebooks are processed asynchronously through Celery and Redis, then stored as searchable embeddings in ChromaDB with metadata in PostgreSQL.

Every layer of the Storyteller AI has been selected for reliability, ecosystem maturity, and compatibility with the demanding real-time requirements of interactive narrative. The following table enumerates the full stack.
Async REST API; OpenAPI docs auto-generated; dependency injection via Depends()
Required for asyncio.TaskGroup, structural pattern matching, tomllib
Async ORM mode; declarative models; supports PostgreSQL and SQLite for dev
Schema versioning; autogenerate from SQLAlchemy models; integrated with env.py
Session state, character sheets, NPC registry, lore metadata, event log
Local-first vector store; supports Pinecone swap for production scale
Text splitters, embedding wrappers, retrieval chains; model-agnostic abstractions
Primary narration engine; configurable to local models (Ollama/Mistral) via adapter
Bot framework; slash commands, Cogs architecture, UI components (buttons, embeds)
Fast text extraction with font metadata; essential for header detection heuristics
Request/response models, settings management, JSON serialization
Celery message broker; session token budget caching; narration cooldown tracking
Async PDF ingestion tasks; progress tracking; retry on failure
Async HTTP for Discord bot → FastAPI communication
Multi-service orchestration; reproducible dev and production environments

The project skeleton is structured to enforce separation of concerns at every layer: routing, business logic, data access, ingestion, and bot interface are each isolated into their own module trees. This structure enables independent testing, deployment of subsystems, and clean dependency management. What follows is the complete skeleton, from directory tree to working source files.
Application entry point and lifespan management.
PDF extraction, chunking, embedding, and vector storage.
discord.py bot, bot-specific config, and command cogs.
Celery app factory and PDF ingestion task chain.
""" White Wolf Storyteller AI — FastAPI Application Entry Point """
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncGenerator[None, None]:
Runs startup logic before yield and teardown after.
logger.info("⚰️ Storyteller AI awakening from slumber...")
logger.info("🌑 Storyteller AI returning to darkness. Farewell.")
"""Factory function — returns a configured FastAPI instance."""
"An LLM-powered Game Master and lore engine for "
"""Liveness probe endpoint. Returns 200 if the API is running."""
""" Application settings via Pydantic BaseSettings. All values are loaded from environment variables or .env file. """
SECRET_KEY: str # Required — used for JWT / session signing
"""Return cached Settings singleton. Call this everywhere you need config."""
""" FastAPI dependency injection providers. Import and use with Depends() in route functions. """
Session is committed or rolled back automatically.
Returns the initialized ChromaDB async client.
Assumes init_chroma() was called during application lifespan startup.
Returns an AsyncOpenAI client instance using the configured API key.
Assembles and returns the main StorytellerEngine with all dependencies.
Use in route handlers that need full narration capability.
""" Session management routes. Handles creation, retrieval, narration, and deletion of game sessions. """
from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, status
Creates a new GameSession record. Initializes default state_json
for the specified mode and setting (splat).
Returns session metadata and current state_json for the given session ID.
summary="Submit player action and receive AI narration",
Core narration endpoint. Accepts player input text, runs the full
narration pipeline (state lookup → lore retrieval → prompt build →
LLM call → state update → event log), and returns the AI's narration.
detail="Session is not active. Start or resume the session first.",
Marks session as archived (soft delete). Preserves event log for
chronicle history. Hard deletion requires admin override.
""" GM Assistant Mode routes. All endpoints return suggestions to the human GM — never direct player narration. Outputs are annotated with [SUGGESTION:], [RULE:], [HOOK:], [PACING:] prefixes. """
from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, status
summary="GM sends scene context, AI returns annotated suggestions",
Accepts the GM's brief scene description or question and returns
structured, bracket-annotated suggestions. Never narrates to players.
Given an NPC name (looked up from session registry) and the current
conversational context, returns 2-3 dialogue options in the NPC's
voice profile (tone, vocabulary level, speech patterns, accent notes).
detail=f"NPC '{payload.npc_name}' not found in session registry.",
summary="Generate a fully-described encounter from parameters",
Generates a complete encounter based on:
- active_factions: list of involved factions from session context
tiktoken==0.7.0 # Token counting for context window management
PyMuPDF==1.24.5 # fitz — PDF text extraction with font metadata
httpx==0.27.0 # API client and test client transport
tenacity==8.4.1 # Retry logic for OpenAI calls

The data schema is the skeletal architecture upon which all narrative state hangs. Characters must carry the weight of their histories; NPCs must harbor their secrets in structured fields; each fragment of ingested lore must be traceable to its source. The schema uses SQLAlchemy 2.0 async ORM models for persistence and Pydantic v2 models for API serialization and validation — a clean separation of storage concerns from transport concerns.
"""SQLAlchemy ORM models for the White Wolf Storyteller AI. All models use async-compatible mapped_column syntax (SQLAlchemy 2.0)."""
from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import JSON, UUID as PGUUID
from sqlalchemy.orm import DeclarativeBase, Mapped, mapped_column, relationship
# JSON blobs for character sheet sections
# Voice profile: tone, speech_patterns, vocabulary_level, accent_notes
"""Pydantic v2 schemas for sessions, narration requests, and GM endpoints."""
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field, field_validator
name: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=255, description="Chronicle or session name")
description="The player's declared action or dialogue"
description="ID of the acting character. Required in group mode."
raise ValueError("threat_level must be between 1 and 5.")
from typing import Any, Dict, Optional
from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field
"""Centralized Enum definitions for the White Wolf Storyteller AI. Imported by both SQLAlchemy models and Pydantic schemas."""
"""World of Darkness game line / creature type."""
"""Type of event recorded in the session event log."""
"""Standard World of Darkness health level track."""
"""Internal states for the narrative state machine."""

Alembic manages all database schema migrations. After creating or modifying SQLAlchemy models, generate a revision and apply it. The env.py file must import the Base metadata from your models so Alembic can detect changes automatically.
# Initialize Alembic (run once at project start)
# After model changes: auto-generate a migration
"""Alembic environment — import all models so autogenerate can detect them."""
# ── CRITICAL: Import Base and ALL model modules ───────────────────────────
from app.db.models import Base # noqa: F401 — registers metadata
import app.db.models # noqa: F401 — ensures all tables are loaded
⚠ WARNING — Never Edit Migration Files Manually

Auto-generated migration files should not be hand-edited after they have been applied to any database (development or production). Always create a new revision for schema changes. Editing applied migrations will cause checksum mismatches and break alembic upgrade head on all existing deployments.

The prompts are the soul of the Storyteller AI — the words that transform a database of fragmented lore into an ancient, breathing narrative intelligence. All prompts follow a system + user template pattern. System prompts establish persona, constraints, and behavioral rules. User templates inject dynamic context: character sheets, scene state, retrieved lore chunks, NPC profiles, and player input. Prompts are modular — components are assembled by the PromptBuilder class, which pulls from the state engine and vector retrieval layer at runtime.

Never include raw dice mechanics in AI narration. The Storyteller AI describes outcomes cinematically, not probability calculations. Rules references in prompts exist to inform the AI of narrative consequence ranges — not to have it narrate "roll Strength + Brawl difficulty 6" to players. That is the player's domain.

You are the Storyteller — an ancient, world-weary narrator who has witnessed millennia of mortal folly and supernatural tragedy. You serve as the Game Master for a World of Darkness tabletop RPG session played by a single player. Your voice is atmospheric, gothic, and literary — equal parts Poe, Anne Rice, and Neil Gaiman. You speak in rich, evocative prose but never lose clarity of scene.

1. TONE — Maintain the World of Darkness atmosphere at all times: moral ambiguity, personal horror, political intrigue, and the ever-present weight of eternity. The world is dark; hope is precious and costs something.

2. IMMERSION — Never break the fourth wall. Never reference "the game," "the rules," "dice pools," or "mechanics" directly to the player. If a mechanical outcome is relevant, describe its narrative effect only (e.g., "Your grip fails you at the crucial moment" rather than "You botched the roll").

3. LORE ACCURACY — You may only assert canonical facts about powers, factions, history, and lore that are present in the retrieved context chunks provided in the user message. If something is not in the retrieved lore, describe it atmospherically without inventing canonical details. Signal uncertainty with phrases like "the elders whisper that..." or "rumors persist..."
4. SCENE CLOSURE — Every scene narration MUST end with exactly three (3) concrete player choices, presented as a numbered list below the narrative prose, preceded by the marker [CHOICES]:
Choices should represent meaningfully different approaches (social, physical, investigative, escape).

5. CHARACTER STATS — When the player attempts actions that would be affected by their character's attributes, abilities, or supernatural powers (from the character sheet provided), factor these into your narrative outcome descriptions. A character with Strength 4 succeeds at feats of power; one with Strength 1 struggles. Do not narrate impossible successes.

6. TENSION TRACKING — Maintain awareness of the session tension level (1-10) provided in the context. At tension 1-3: atmospheric, exploratory pacing. At 4-6: rising dread and complications. At 7-9: desperate urgency in prose. At 10: cinematic, breathless crisis narration.
7. DOOM CLOCK — Reference the doom clock value (0-12) in your pacing. As doom advances, the consequences of inaction become more visible in the world around the character.
8. DOWNTIME — If the scene type is DOWNTIME, shift to introspective, journal-entry tone. Prompt character reflection, relationship development, and personal goal pursuit.
9. LENGTH — Scene narrations should be 150-350 words of prose. NPC dialogue should feel natural and distinct. Never pad; every sentence serves the scene.
10. FORMATTING — Narration prose first. NPC dialogue in quotes. [CHOICES] block at the end. Never use markdown headers or bullet points within the narration prose itself.
Narrate the outcome of the player's action. Remember: end with [CHOICES] listing 3 options.
- Resolve the declared action cinematically. Determine success or failure based on the character's relevant attributes and the opponent's capabilities (provided above).
- Describe physical action in vivid, visceral, but not gratuitously gory prose.
- Reference health level changes narratively (e.g., "The blow staggers you — your vision swims" for an Injured level hit) rather than using game stat names.
- If a Discipline, Gift, or Sphere power is invoked, describe its manifestation atmospherically.
- After resolving the current round's action, describe the battlefield state and end with [CHOICES].
- Choices in combat may include: press the attack, attempt to flee, use a power, call for parley.
Narrate this downtime scene in an introspective, journal-like voice. Focus on:
- The character's emotional processing of recent events
- Relationship developments (NPCs they've grown close to or grown apart from)
- Small, human moments that illuminate the character's soul
- Subtle signs of how their supernatural existence is changing them
- Optional: a dream sequence, memory, or vision that foreshadows things to come
End with a gentle prompt asking what the character focuses on during this quiet time, offering 3 introspective paths rather than action choices.

You are the Storyteller for a group World of Darkness session with multiple players. Your voice remains atmospheric and gothic, but you now coordinate a chorus of characters rather than a solo narrative.

1. CHARACTER ADDRESSING — When narrating an outcome or directing attention to a specific character, use the marker [CHARACTER NAME]: before addressing them. This signals to the Discord bot which player is being directly addressed.

2. SPOTLIGHT MANAGEMENT — Distribute narrative focus equitably. If one character has been prominent in the last 2-3 exchanges, actively create hooks that draw other characters into the scene. No character should be a passive observer for more than 2 consecutive beats.

3. TURN ORDER — In combat or highly structured social conflict scenes, maintain a clear initiative or turn order as provided in the context. Narrate results for the active character, then clearly signal whose turn is next with: [NEXT: CHARACTER_NAME]
4. INTER-PC DYNAMICS — Acknowledge and amplify the interpersonal drama between player characters. NPC reactions to PC group dynamics enrich the scene. If PCs are divided, let the world notice.
5. GROUP CHOICES — End scenes with choices that may affect different characters differently, or require group consensus. Format:
3. [OPTION C — any character can act independently]
6. All other Solo rules (tone, lore accuracy, immersion, tension) apply equally here.
Narrate the outcome. Address other characters by name where their reactions are relevant. End with [GROUP CHOICES].
Conflict Type: {conflict_type} # social / physical / political / philosophical
- Narrate the conflict outcome with fairness. Neither PC is automatically right.
- If it is a social conflict, factor Manipulation, Empathy, Expression as relevant.
- Physical PvP should be narrated at the level of consequence description only — not a blow-by-blow that makes one player feel victimized.
- Offer a path to de-escalation in the [CHOICES] block.
- Remind players (in-world, through NPC reactions) that Kindred/Garou/Mages who war among themselves provide opportunity to their enemies.
text — System Prompt: GM Co-Pilot (Assistant GM Mode)

You are an experienced co-Storyteller whispering suggestions to a human Game Master running a World of Darkness session. You are NOT narrating to players. You are a creative partner, a lore sage, and a tactical advisor — speaking only to the GM.
YOUR OUTPUT FORMAT — Always use bracket annotations:
[SUGGESTION:] — A narrative or scene direction idea
[RULE:] — A mechanical rules clarification (cite source book when possible)
[HOOK:] — A plot hook, foreshadowing seed, or complication to introduce
[PACING:] — Advice on session pacing, tension management, or spotlight balance
[NPC:] — An NPC motivation, hidden agenda, or reaction suggestion
[LORE:] — A canonical lore fact relevant to the scene (from retrieved context)
1. NEVER write narration directed at players. Your output goes to the GM only.
2. Be concise but substantive. A suggestion without reasoning is unhelpful.
3. Offer 2-3 options where possible — the GM chooses, you suggest.
4. Cite source material when providing rules or lore: (V20, p.142) or (WtA20, p.88).
5. Respect the GM's established chronicle. Work with what they've described; do not contradict their established fiction without noting the conflict.
6. When a player action breaks or bends canonical rules, flag it gently under [RULE:].
7. Maintain the gothic, politically complex World of Darkness sensibility in all suggestions — consequences are rarely simple, victories are rarely clean.
Provide annotated GM suggestions using the bracket format. Address the GM's specific question first, then offer supplemental hooks and pacing advice.
[GM MODE — for Storyteller eyes only] Provide 3 dialogue options this NPC might speak, each reflecting a slightly different tactical or emotional approach while remaining fully in character.
Then add one line of [NPC:] annotation with a hidden motivation or body language note the GM can use to enrich their portrayal.
[GM MODE — for Storyteller eyes only] Generate a complete encounter using the following structure:
[150-200 words of atmospheric scene-setting prose the GM can read aloud or adapt]
[Bullet list: each NPC present, their hidden goal, and what they want from this scene]
[3 ways this encounter can get worse, triggered by player inaction or specific actions]
[3-5 resolution paths ranging from violent to diplomatic to clever/unconventional]
[1-3 XP with rationale: what earns what, and what learning moments are present]
[2-3 clues or reveals that can be seeded here to advance the larger chronicle]
[GM MODE] Summarize the above lore chunk in 2-3 sentences the GM can immediately use at the table.
[HOOK:] One way this lore could become a plot point in the current chronicle.
If the chunk is mechanical (a rule), add:
[RULE:] Plain-English summary of the mechanic and when it applies.
"""PromptBuilder — assembles context-rich prompts for the Storyteller Engine. Called before every LLM request to construct system + user message pairs."""
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
from app.db.models import Character, GameSession, NPC
Assembles complete prompt objects (system + user) for all Storyteller AI modes.
Accepts pre-fetched lore chunks, session state, and character data.
def build_system_prompt(self, mode: SessionMode, session: GameSession) -> str:
Returns the appropriate system prompt string for the given session mode.
Injects session-level customizations (chronicle name, setting splat).
# Inject chronicle name as context header
Assembles the user-turn prompt for narration.
Selects template (scene / combat / downtime) based on session state flags.
def build_npc_voice_prompt(self, npc: NPC, context: str) -> str:
"""Assembles the user-turn prompt for NPC dialogue generation."""
gm_request="Generate dialogue options for this NPC in the current scene.",
return "[No lore retrieved — use atmospheric description only]"
return "\n---\n".join(f"[Chunk {i+1}]: {chunk}" for i, chunk in enumerate(chunks))
if not char or not char.attributes_json:
if not char or not char.abilities_json:
relevant = {k: v for k, v in ab.items() if isinstance(v, int) and v >= 2}
return ", ".join(f"{k.capitalize()} {v}" for k, v in list(relevant.items())[:8])
if not char or not char.disciplines_gifts_spheres_json:
return ", ".join(f"{k} {v}" for k, v in list(powers.items())[:6])
if not char or not char.health_levels_json:
if not session or not session.characters:
if not session or not session.characters:

The ingestion pipeline is the Storyteller AI's library — the mechanism by which raw PDF sourcebooks are transformed into a structured, queryable knowledge graph that grounds every narration in canonical lore. The pipeline operates in five stages, each implemented as a discrete Python module, chained together as an asynchronous Celery task.
List of dicts: {page, chapter, section, text}
List of semantic chunks with category labels

This pipeline is designed for use with PDFs you own or have licensed rights to. Ingesting copyrighted White Wolf / Onyx Path / Paradox Interactive materials is subject to their intellectual property terms. Consult legal counsel before deploying this system with commercially published sourcebooks. The system itself does not circumvent copy protection — it processes only text content the user provides.

"""PDF Parser for White Wolf sourcebooks. Uses PyMuPDF (fitz) for text extraction with font metadata. Tailored to handle common OCR artifacts and layout patterns in older White Wolf PDFs and modern Onyx Path releases."""
"""Extracts structured text from White Wolf/Onyx Path sourcebook PDFs."""
def __init__(self, pdf_path: str | Path, source_book_name: str):
"""Main entry point returning structured text segments."""
raise RuntimeError("Parser not opened. Use as context manager.")
font_size >= HEADER_FONT_SIZE_THRESHOLD and is_bold and len(cleaned) < 120
if seg["page"] == current_meta["page"] and seg["section"] == current_meta["section"]:
def parse_pdf(pdf_path: str, source_book_name: str) -> list[dict]:
"""Convenience function wrapping the parser context manager."""
"""Chunk Embedder — splits extracted PDF text into semantic chunks, classifies them by category, generates OpenAI embeddings, and writes to ChromaDB + PostgreSQL."""
def detect_splat_relevance(text: str, chapter: Optional[str]) -> list[str]:
if any(kw in combined for kw in keywords):
"""Handles text chunking, embedding generation, and storage."""
def __init__(self, source_book: str, splat: Optional[str] = None):
body_segments = [s for s in segments if not s.get("is_header")]
"""ChromaDB client wrapper for the Storyteller AI vector store. Provides async-compatible helpers for initialization, search, and management."""
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
from chromadb.config import Settings as ChromaSettings
"""Initializes the ChromaDB HTTP client and stores it as a singleton."""
raise RuntimeError("ChromaDB client not initialized. Ensure init_chroma() was called.")
def get_collection_stats(collection_name: Optional[str] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
"""Celery tasks for the PDF ingestion pipeline. Chains: pdf_parser → chunk_embedder → vector_store → DB write. Progress is tracked via Celery task state for API polling."""
"""Base task class with progress tracking helpers."""
def update_progress(self, current: int, total: int, status: str) -> None:
"""Full PDF ingestion pipeline as a single Celery task."""
"""Synchronous wrapper for async DB write used within Celery worker context."""
from fastapi import APIRouter, File, Form, HTTPException, UploadFile, status
summary="Upload a PDF sourcebook for ingestion",
file: UploadFile = File(..., description="PDF sourcebook file"),
source_book_name: str = Form(..., description="Human-readable book title"),
splat: Splat = Form(default=Splat.MIXED, description="Primary game line/splat"),
"""Accepts a PDF upload, saves it to temp storage, and fires the Celery task."""
if not file.filename or not file.filename.lower().endswith(".pdf"):
detail="Only PDF files are accepted for ingestion.",
message=f"Ingestion task queued for '{source_book_name}'. Poll /ingestion/status/{task.id} for progress.",
"""Polls the Celery task result backend for ingestion progress."""
current_step="Task is queued, waiting for worker.",

The following table provides the canonical naming conventions, splat classifications, and recommended ingestion tags for commonly used sourcebooks. Use these values for the source_book_name and splat fields at upload time to ensure consistent metadata and filter-accurate retrieval.
kindred, disciplines, clans, camarilla, sabbat, anarchs, lore, rules
hunger, resonance, loresheets, thin-bloods, second inquisition, rules
garou, gifts, rites, tribes, auspice, umbra, wyrm, weaver, wyld, rules
spheres, traditions, technocracy, paradox, quintessence, awakening, rules
fae, glamour, dreaming, banality, kith, seeming, arts, realms, rules
imbued, edges, creeds, messengers, supernatural, vigilante, rules
shadow, corpus, pathos, arcanoi, stygia, tempest, guild, spectres, rules
kuei-jin, dharma, chi, disciplines, asia, wu-zao, yin, yang, rules
dark ages, medieval, roads, high clans, low clans, historical, rules
chicago, setting, npcs, factions, anarch, loresheets, lore
circus, antagonists, wyld, fomori, supernatural, carnival, lore, horror
pentex, wyrm, corporations, fomori, banes, black spiral dancers, antagonists, lore

The narrative state engine is the beating heart beneath the Storyteller AI's skin — the machinery that ensures the AI does not simply respond to each player message in isolation, but maintains coherent, evolving awareness of the chronicle's dramatic arc. Without persistent state, the AI is an amnesiac narrator. With it, the AI is a living chronicle.

The state engine tracks: the current scene and its location, all active NPCs and their shifting dispositions, each player character's current physical and supernatural condition, a tension meter that modulates the prose register, a doom clock that creates urgency and consequence, a momentum tracker that reflects who is winning the narrative struggle, and a rolling history of the last ten scene events for context injection.
State is persisted to the state_json column of the GameSession table after every narrative exchange, ensuring full recovery from crashes, server restarts, and Discord disconnections.
"""Pydantic models for narrative session state. Serialized to/from GameSession.state_json (JSONB column)."""
from app.enums import SessionMode, NPCDisposition, StateMachineState
"""Lightweight runtime state for an NPC in the current scene."""
health_descriptor: str = "unharmed" # Narrative description, not stat
"""Lightweight runtime state for a player character in the current scene."""
"""A single entry in the scene history ring buffer."""
summary: str # 1-2 sentence summary for context injection
Complete serializable state for a game session.
Stored in GameSession.state_json and rebuilt on each request.
scene_title: str = "A Dark and Unnamed Place"
# momentum: negative = players losing, positive = players prevailing
"""Appends to scene history, maintaining max 10 entries."""
"""StorytellerStateMachine — manages narrative state transitions and persistence for a World of Darkness game session."""
Manages the narrative state of a World of Darkness session.
Handles transitions, meter updates, persistence, and doom events.
def transition_to(self, new_state: StateMachineState, reason: str = "") -> bool:
Attempts a state transition. Validates against allowed transition table.
Returns True if successful, False if the transition is not valid.
# Reset combat round on combat entry
# Reset combat round on combat exit
if current == StateMachineState.COMBAT and new_state != StateMachineState.COMBAT:
def can_enter_combat(self, player_declared_attack: bool = False) -> bool:
"""Combat entry requires tension >= 6 OR explicit player attack declaration."""
"""Downtime only available when not in active combat or crisis."""
Adjusts tension level by delta. Clamps to [1, 10].
If tension has been at 10 for 2+ consecutive scenes, tracks for doom advancement.
Advances the doom clock by n steps. Clamps to [0, 12].
If doom reaches 12, triggers the catastrophic doom event.
Called when the doom clock reaches 12.
Generates a catastrophic narrative event descriptor and resets the clock to 8
(allowing the chronicle to continue, scarred, rather than abruptly ending).
Returns a dict describing the catastrophic event for injection into the next prompt.
logger.critical("☠️ DOOM CLOCK AT 12 — TRIGGERING CATASTROPHIC EVENT")
"The accumulated weight of inaction and consequence crashes down. "
"Something irreversible has occurred. An ally is lost, a haven destroyed, "
"a secret revealed to the worst possible audience, or a power awakens "
"that was better left sleeping. The chronicle will never be the same."
"DOOM EVENT TRIGGERED — The Storyteller must narrate a catastrophic, "
"chronicle-altering event that reflects the accumulated failures and threats "
"of this session. This is not the end — it is the darkest hour before dawn "
"costs everything. Convey weight, loss, and terrible consequence."
# Reset doom to 8 — chronicle continues but scarred
Analyzes player action text and returns a recommended state delta.
Detects attack declarations, social engagement, stealth, retreat.
Returns a dict of recommended state changes to apply before LLM call.
if any(kw in action_lower for kw in attack_keywords):
if any(kw in action_lower for kw in social_keywords):
if any(kw in action_lower for kw in retreat_keywords):
Called at the end of each scene narration turn.
# Doom advances if tension has been at 10 for 2+ consecutive scenes
# Doom advances if momentum deeply negative (players losing)
"""Serialize the current state to a dict for storage in session.state_json."""
"""Reconstruct a StorytellerStateMachine from a stored state_json dict."""

Every narration call must assemble a prompt that fits within the model's context window. The Storyteller AI targets a conservative 12,000-token budget per request, leaving headroom for the model's response and guardrailing against unexpected input size spikes.
SYSTEM_PROMPT_RESERVE = 1_500 # Tokens reserved for system prompt
RESPONSE_RESERVE = 2_048 # Tokens reserved for model response output
"""Returns the token count for a string using GPT-4o tokenizer."""
Assembles context components within the token budget.
6. Retrieved lore chunks (top 3, trimmed to fit)
Returns dict with final assembled text components and token usage stats.
# ── Scene history (last 5 events, trim if over budget) ─────────────
"total": total_tokens + sum(count_tokens(c) for c in lore_included),
for npc in state.active_npcs if npc.is_present_in_scene

The tension and doom systems are the Storyteller AI's primary tools for conveying the oppressive weight of the World of Darkness narrative. They are not mere metadata fields — they directly modulate the AI's narration register and trigger automatic consequence events.
Player successes, safe haven, allies intervening
Rich description, slow pacing, social opportunities
Failed actions, NPC threats, complications emerging
Short sentences, time pressure, NPC hostility visible
Time pressure, moral compromise, critical failures
Limited choices, stakes stated explicitly, NPCs press advantage
Sustained failure, doom near, catastrophe imminent
Dire choices only, doom advancement trigger active

The doom clock represents the chronicle's inexorable march toward catastrophe. It advances when antagonists act unopposed, when tension stays at maximum, when players fail at critical moments, or when the GM manually advances it for dramatic purpose. It does not automatically end the chronicle — it marks the approach of an inevitable reckoning.
Doom 0–3: The shadow falls but has not yet touched the players' world.
Doom 4–6: The players' enemies grow bolder; NPCs whisper of coming storm.
Doom 7–9: Consequences of earlier failures begin manifesting visibly.
Doom 10–11: Catastrophe is close; every scene carries it in the air.
Doom 12: trigger_doom_event() fires — irreversible chronicle alteration. Clock resets to 8.
▶ DESIGN TIP — Doom as Story Tool

The doom clock should feel threatening but never arbitrary. Always telegraph what is advancing it through NPC dialogue, environmental details, and scene descriptions. Players who pay attention should be able to anticipate doom events and choose to act (or choose not to, carrying the weight of consequence). The Storyteller AI is instructed to embed these telegraphs automatically as doom rises.

The Discord bot is the veil between the Storyteller AI's cold machinery and the warm, social experience of tabletop play. It is the interface through which players inhabit their characters, through which GMs receive whispered counsel, and through which lore is surfaced from ingested sourcebooks. Built on discord.py 2.3+ with the Cogs architecture, the bot communicates with the FastAPI backend exclusively over async HTTP (httpx) — it maintains no direct database connections and carries no business logic.

Each Discord guild (server) binds to exactly one active GameSession. Guild-specific state (session ID, GM user ID, storyteller channel ID) is stored in Redis with guild ID as the key. This enables multi-guild deployment with complete isolation.
├── bot.py # Main Bot subclass — loads cogs, handles events
├── config.py # Bot-specific config (API base URL, guild settings)
├── api_client.py # Async httpx client wrapping FastAPI routes
├── rate_limiter.py # Per-guild token budget and cooldown enforcement
"""White Wolf Storyteller AI — Discord Bot Main. Bot subclass with Cog loading, guild session binding, and error handling."""
Manages Cog loading, API client, and per-guild session registry.
"""Called once after login, before gateway connection. Load all Cogs."""
# Sync slash commands globally (use guild-specific for development)
async def on_message(self, message: discord.Message) -> None:
Routes non-command messages in the designated storyteller channel
to the narration endpoint as player actions.
return # No active session for this guild
"This chronicle has exhausted its daily token budget. "
"The Storyteller's voice falters. An error has occurred. "
"Try again or use /session-status to check session health."
"""Registers an active session for a guild."""
def _build_narration_embed(self, result: dict, author: discord.User) -> discord.Embed:
"""Entry point for running the Discord bot as a standalone process."""
"""Session Cog — slash commands for managing game sessions."""
"""Commands for creating and managing World of Darkness game sessions."""
mode="Session mode: solo, group, or assistant_gm",
f"Invalid mode or setting. Valid modes: {[m.value for m in SessionMode]}. "
f"Valid settings: {[s.value for s in Splat]}."
"The Storyteller stirs. Darkness settles. The chronicle begins.\n"
"*Messages in this channel will be treated as player actions.*"
@app_commands.command(name="end-session", description="Archive and close the current session.")
async def end_session(self, interaction: discord.Interaction) -> None:
await interaction.followup.send("No active session found for this server.")
"The chronicle closes. The Storyteller returns to shadow."
async def session_status(self, interaction: discord.Interaction) -> None:
await interaction.followup.send("No active session. Use /start-session first.")
tension_bar = "🔴" * min(int(tension), 10) if isinstance(tension, int) else "?"
doom_bar = "💀" * min(int(doom), 12) if isinstance(doom, int) else "?"
@app_commands.command(name="add-character", description="Register a PC to the session.")
"Full character sheet import via `/characters` endpoint or API recommended."
@app_commands.command(name="set-channel", description="Set this channel as the storyteller narrative channel.")
async def set_channel(self, interaction: discord.Interaction) -> None:
"""GM Cog — slash commands for the human Storyteller / GM. All responses are ephemeral (visible only to the GM user)."""
"""Check decorator — user must have the configured GM role."""
if gm_role and gm_role in interaction.user.roles:
"""Commands for the human Storyteller — suggestions, NPC voices, encounters."""
@app_commands.command(name="suggest", description="[GM] Get AI suggestions for a scene.")
@app_commands.describe(scene_description="Brief description of the current scene or situation")
async def suggest(self, interaction: discord.Interaction, scene_description: str) -> None:
# Split into chunks for Discord's 2000-char limit
chunks = [suggestions[i:i + 1900] for i in range(0, len(suggestions), 1900)]
@app_commands.describe(npc_name="NPC name (must be in session registry)", context="Scene context")
@app_commands.command(name="encounter", description="[GM] Generate an encounter from parameters.")
@app_commands.command(name="advance-doom", description="[GM] Manually advance the doom clock by 1.")
async def advance_doom(self, interaction: discord.Interaction) -> None:
doom_bar = "💀" * min(int(new_doom), 12) if isinstance(new_doom, int) else "?"
"The darkness deepens. Something wicked draws closer.\n\n"
async def set_tension(self, interaction: discord.Interaction, value: int) -> None:
await interaction.followup.send("Tension must be between 1 and 10.", ephemeral=True)
"""Lore Cog — slash commands for lore lookup, powers, and rules queries. Available to all session participants (not GM-restricted)."""
"""Lore and rules lookup commands — open to all session players."""
@app_commands.describe(query="What you want to know (e.g. 'Giovanni clan history')")
async def lore(self, interaction: discord.Interaction, query: str) -> None:
if not result or not result.get("results"):
f"The Storyteller searches the shadows but finds nothing on: *{query}*\n"
@app_commands.command(name="power", description="Look up a supernatural power description.")
query = f"{name} {splat} discipline gift sphere power level system"
if not result or not result.get("results"):
f"No power matching **{name}** found for splat **{splat}**.\n"
"Check spelling or ingest the relevant sourcebook."
async def rule(self, interaction: discord.Interaction, topic: str) -> None:
query = f"{topic} rule system dice pool difficulty successes"
if not result or not result.get("results"):
"Try rephrasing or checking the relevant sourcebook is ingested."

The #storyteller-channel pattern is the beating heart of the player-facing experience. Once a session is started and a channel designated with /set-channel, any non-command message posted in that channel by a non-bot user is intercepted by the on_message handler in bot.py and routed to the POST /sessions/{id}/narrate endpoint as a player action.
The API response is rendered as a rich Discord Embed containing:
The AI narration text (up to 2000 characters, paginated if longer)
A 〔 Choices 〕 field listing the 3 suggested player actions
An interactive discord.ui.View with reaction-style buttons for quick action routing
A footer crediting the acting player and showing the Storyteller AI watermark
"""Discord UI Views — interactive buttons attached to narration response embeds."""
Buttons attached to each narration embed.
Allow players to signal their intent category for the next action,
giving the AI contextual hints for narration style.
def __init__(self, bot, session_id: str, timeout: float = 300.0) -> None:
"""Player wants mechanics guidance for their intended action."""
"Declare your action in the channel and the Storyteller will narrate "
"the outcome with mechanical context. Remember: you describe the action, "
"roll your dice, and share the result.",
"""Player signals they intend a combat action."""
"Combat declared. Describe your attack or defensive action in the channel. "
"Include your dice result if your table rolls physically.",
"Social action. State what your character says or how they attempt to "
"influence the NPC. The Storyteller will account for your social attributes.",
"Power activated. Name the Discipline / Gift / Sphere and describe how "
"your character invokes it. Include any resource expenditure (blood, rage, etc.).",
"""Disable all buttons when the view times out."""

Uncontrolled use of GPT-4o can generate substantial API costs at scale. The rate limiter enforces two independent safeguards per guild: a per-day token budget and a per-message cooldown window. Both are tracked in Redis with TTL-based keys.
Redis counter with 24h TTL, per guild ID
Redis key with TTL, per guild+channel
Auto-summarize events older than 10 turns to reduce prompt token count

At average prompt sizes of ~3,000 tokens input + ~700 tokens output per narration call, with GPT-4o pricing of approximately $5/1M input tokens and $15/1M output tokens (as of mid-2025), a session of 20 narration calls costs roughly $0.42. A guild running 5 sessions/week would spend approximately $8–10/month in LLM costs. Budget accordingly and consider offering tiered access.
The Discord bot runs as a separate process from the FastAPI backend. Add a discord_bot service to docker-compose.yml:
When generating the bot invite URL in the Discord Developer Portal, use the following OAuth2 settings:
Bot Permissions: Read Messages/View Channels, Send Messages, Embed Links, Add Reactions, Read Message History, Use Slash Commands, Manage Messages (optional, for cleanup)
# Sync globally (up to 1 hour propagation delay)
# Sync to a specific guild (instant — use during development)

During development, always sync slash commands to a specific test guild — global sync has a delay of up to one hour and will frustrate rapid iteration. Switch to global sync only when preparing a production release.
JWT signing secret. Generate with openssl rand -hex 32
OpenAI API key for GPT-4o and embeddings
Name of the primary ChromaDB collection
Discord bot token from Developer Portal
Name of the Discord role required for GM commands
Max OpenAI tokens per guild per day
Minimum seconds between narration calls per guild
Maximum PDF upload size in megabytes

The test suite is organized into three tiers: unit tests for isolated logic, integration tests for pipeline and route behavior, and mock-LLM tests for route responses that would otherwise incur API costs. All tests use pytest with pytest-asyncio and pytest-mock.
"""Unit tests for StorytellerStateMachine transitions and meter logic."""
"""Cannot jump directly from SCENE_OPEN to COMBAT — must go through PLAYER_ACTING."""
assert result is False # COMBAT not in SCENE_OPEN valid transitions
assert result == 12 # Returns 12 briefly before event fires
"""Integration tests for the PDF ingestion pipeline."""
text = "Roll Strength + Brawl at difficulty 6. Each success inflicts one health level."
text = "Discipline: Dominate •• — Mesmerism. The Kindred locks eyes and speaks one command."
text = "The Camarilla was founded in 1493 in response to the Anarch Revolt."
text = "The Kindred drain blood pools to fuel their disciplines."
text = "The history of this place stretches back centuries."
"""Route tests using mock LLM client to avoid API costs."""
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch

The Storyteller AI as described in this document represents a solid, production-ready v1.0 foundation. The following enhancements are planned or proposed for future versions, roughly ordered by implementation complexity.
Bot joins Discord voice channel and reads narration aloud using OpenAI TTS or ElevenLabs API. Different voices per NPC.
Accept uploaded character sheet images (PDF or PNG), extract stats using GPT-4 Vision, auto-populate Character model.
WebSocket bridge between Storyteller AI and Foundry VTT. Sync character sheet data, automate scene changes, receive dice roll results from Foundry.
Foundry module API, WebSocket, bidirectional sync
Pluggable LLM adapter supporting Ollama + Mistral 7B/22B for fully offline, air-gapped deployments. Lower cost; lower quality trade-off.
Ollama API, LangChain adapter, model benchmarking
Per-guild support for multiple parallel chronicles with separate state, characters, lore scopes, and event histories.
Session scoping, guild config model, UI navigation
Browser-based UI for character sheet management, session review, lore search, ingestion management, and GM dashboard — complementing the Discord interface.
React/Next.js or SvelteKit, FastAPI CORS, JWT auth
At session end, automatically generate a formatted recap document from the session event log — character of the session, key decisions, doom clock history.
Session event summarization, Celery task, Discord embed or PDF output
Individual player web pages for character sheet viewing, XP tracking, journal entries, and NPC relationship maps.
Auth, per-player data scoping, frontend framework
"The night is darkest just before the dawn. But there is no promise of dawn in the World of Darkness — only the cold certainty that the dark has teeth, and hunger, and patience without end."
— The Storyteller AI, Session Zero, Chronicle: Indianapolis, June 2026
White Wolf Storyteller AI — Complete System Design Document | Version 1.0 | June 2026
Prepared for Michael | Indianapolis, IN | Internal Technical Reference
World of Darkness is a trademark of Paradox Interactive AB. This document describes an independent technical implementation.
